created_by: v14 init (Gao framework, Architecture Tool Universe)
description: Closed universe of 20 LLM-architectural-modification primitives. Every step 05 candidate (v14+) must specify which slot(s) it modifies (1+). A candidate with no slot assignment is rejected at step 05.
source: Distilled from Gao ACL-2025 keynote prescription for niche-mining-style benchmarks: define a closed solution universe so candidates are slot-able and comparable.

slots.name: modify_attention_scoring_function
slots.description: Modify the attention scoring function (replace softmax with alternative: quadratic, kernel-based, sub-quadratic, sparse). Examples: Performer (random-feature kernel), Linformer (low-rank projection), Reformer (LSH-bucket softmax).

slots.name: change_normalization_placement
slots.description: Move LayerNorm pre- vs post-residual; add new normalization layer; change normalization type (LayerNorm, RMSNorm, BatchNorm). Examples: GPT-2 to GPT-3 (post-norm to pre-norm).

slots.name: add_gating_module
slots.description: Add a gating mechanism on hidden state, attention output, or FFN output. Gate has its own learnable parameters. Examples: GLU, gated MLP, gated residual.

slots.name: modify_positional_encoding
slots.description: Replace absolute or rotary positional encoding with new variant. Examples: ALiBi, RoPE, Sinusoidal, Learned absolute, Relative bias.

slots.name: change_residual_structure
slots.description: Modify residual connection topology: dense connections, skip patterns, deep-supervision, normalized residual. Examples: ResNet, DenseNet, ReZero, Highway Networks.

slots.name: modify_softmax_function
slots.description: Replace standard softmax with alternative: entmax, sparsemax, combinatorial discrete, Gumbel-softmax, temperature-modulated. Different from S01 (S01 modifies attention SCORING; S06 modifies the softmax NORMALIZATION step). Examples: Sparsemax, Entmax, Top-k softmax.

slots.name: add_sparsity_constraint
slots.description: Add structural sparsity: block-sparse, lottery-ticket pruned, magnitude-pruned, structured-zero patterns. Examples: BigBird, Longformer, Sparse Transformer.

slots.name: modify_FFN_structure
slots.description: Modify feed-forward network: MoE routing, expert sub-networks, low-rank decomposition, factorized FFN. Examples: Switch Transformer, GShard, GLaM.

slots.name: add_new_learnable_module
slots.description: Insert a new learnable sub-module: side-network, adapter, prefix-tuning, LoRA, prompt-tuning. The module has its own parameters separate from baseline weights. Examples: Adapter, LoRA, Prefix-Tuning, side-network.

slots.name: modify_layer_depth_structure
slots.description: Variable depth, early-exit, layer skipping, ALBERT-style cross-layer sharing. Examples: ALBERT (cross-layer sharing), early-exit BERT, deep equilibrium models.

slots.name: add_inter_layer_pathway
slots.description: Add new cross-layer connection: highway, gated skip, attention over layers. Different from S05 (which modifies the EXISTING residual). S11 ADDS a NEW pathway. Examples: Highway Networks, layer-attention.

slots.name: modify_embedding_structure
slots.description: Replace token embedding with new structure: learned codebook, retrieval-augmented embedding, factorized embedding. Examples: ALBERT factorized embedding, VQ-VAE codebook.

slots.name: add_external_memory_module
slots.description: Add external memory: RAG, kNN-LM, episodic memory, retrieval-augmented. The memory is queried at inference time. Examples: RAG, kNN-LM, Memorizing Transformer.

slots.name: modify_training_objective
slots.description: Add new training-time loss: contrastive, distillation, gradient-matching, dropout-as-regularization. Examples: SimCSE, knowledge distillation, R-Drop.

slots.name: add_discriminator_or_critic
slots.description: Add discriminator/critic head for adversarial training or RL. Examples: GAN-discriminator, RLHF reward model, ELECTRA discriminator.

slots.name: modify_token_routing
slots.description: Token-level routing or skipping: MoE token routing, capsules, modular sub-networks per token. Examples: MoE expert routing, Switch Transformer routing, AdaTape.

slots.name: add_recurrence_or_state
slots.description: Add recurrence, hidden state, or memory cell: RWKV, Mamba, S4, Linear RNN. Stateful inference. Examples: Mamba, RWKV, S4, RetNet, Linear RNN.

slots.name: modify_head_structure
slots.description: Modify multi-head attention structure: head count, head specialization, hierarchical heads, grouped-query attention. Examples: Multi-Query Attention, Grouped-Query Attention, Cross-Head, Multi-Token Attention.

slots.name: add_equivariance_constraint
slots.description: Add geometric or algebraic equivariance constraint: SE(2)-equivariant, SU(2)-equivariant, permutation-equivariant, group-equivariant. The constraint forces architectural invariance under group action. Examples: SU(2)-equivariant module, Lie-group attention, permutation-invariant pooling.

slots.name: modify_inference_time_compute
slots.description: Modify inference-time compute: MCTS, beam, sampling temperature scheduling, test-time training, draft-verification. Examples: Speculative Decoding, Medusa, Test-time training.

non_slots: parametrization-tweak (e.g., R279-style integer-ratio harmonic regularizer; rejected at step 05.5)

expected_slot_distribution_uniform_prior.note: Under uniform prior, 25 selected candidates with 1 slot each gives 1.25 per slot on average (= 25/20).

architecturally_deep_slot_rationale: These slots represent modifications that are most likely to produce architecturally-distinguishable candidates (per E31/E32 evidence: R756 SU(2)-equiv [S19], R770 tropical [S01/S06], R777 quiver [S09/S11], R787 crystal-basis [S01/S06]). The max_over_100 projection up-weights candidates in these slots.
v14_observation: The 20-slot universe is the operational closure for niche-mining. A candidate that genuinely modifies no slot is metaphor-only by Gao's framework, and v14 rejects it at step 05.
extension_policy: v14 freezes the universe at 20 slots. Extensions to 21+ slots are forbidden mid-epoch. v15 may extend the universe based on observed coverage patterns.
